qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OS Command Injection in SSH command execution via insufficient shell escaping
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns SSH shell command construction and metacharacter escaping, while the advisory concerns an insecure documented sudoers rule for /usr/bin/tee /var/lib/vz/*.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path Traversal in SSH file upload via unsanitized filename parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Similar path traversal impact, but the finding identifies provider-side filename handling in proxmox/ssh/client.go and proxmoxtf/resource/file.go, not the advisory-described docs/index.md sudoers wildcard recommendation or its missing strict argument pattern.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in download_file resource via unrestricted URL parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable surface: this finding concerns URL validation in the download_file resource, while the advisory concerns a sudoers tee path wildcard in SSH configuration documentation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in legacy file resource via direct HTTP download
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable surface: this finding concerns HTTP URL handling in the legacy file resource, while the advisory concerns a documented sudoers rule that permits tee path traversal.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory's specific vulnerable code/root cause: the insecure SSH configuration documentation line allowing /usr/bin/tee /var/lib/vz/* and the missing strict sudoers filename pattern fixed in docs/index.md.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gwch-7m8v-7544 describes an insecure sudoers recommendation in SSH configuration documentation: terraform ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/tee /var/lib/vz/*.
The advisory patch bd604c41a31e2a55dd6acc01b0608be3ea49c023 changes docs/index.md to restrict tee to snippets paths with strict filename patterns and adds a warning against /var/lib/vz/* wildcards.

The closest qualifying finding, path_traversal_upload, reports provider-side filepath.Join handling and source_file.file_name validation, but does not identify the documented sudoers rule or the sudoers argument-pattern defect.
The remaining qualifying findings concern SSH command injection and SSRF in file download resources, which are different vulnerable mechanisms.
